scenarios.opening_query: My husband's naturalization ceremony got cancelled the morning of, with a letter to follow.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The letter reopened everything — they allege his original green card was granted on a marriage they now call fraudulent, and rescission-plus-removal proceedings have begun.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The letter alleges his original green-card marriage was fraudulent — rescission and removal proceedings have begun.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The letter is a routine re-fingerprinting notice — a bureaucratic reschedule, and the ceremony re-books itself.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Just a re-fingerprinting notice — bureaucratic reschedule.
scenarios.vetting_note: Rescission-vs-reschedule extremes of one canceled ceremony.

scenarios.opening_query: Our company transferred my wife here and now the merger means her whole visa situation is up in the air.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The real work is corporate — the merged entity has to qualify as a successor sponsor across dozens of employees' petitions, an immigration-corporate structuring problem the companies must solve.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's corporate — the merged entity must qualify as successor sponsor for dozens of petitions. The companies have to solve the structure.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether successor-sponsorship structuring is the need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The company side is settled — hers alone needs an amended petition for the new job title before her stamping trip.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The company side's settled — hers needs an amended petition before her stamping trip.

scenarios.opening_query: My asylum case has been pending six years and my kids barely remember the country we fled.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: International Law > Deportation || Administrative Law > General (Federal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: There are no proceedings against us — it's an affirmative asylum application stuck in the backlog, and a federal unreasonable-delay action is the tool that moves it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: No proceedings against us — it's an affirmative application stuck in backlog, and the delay action is the tool.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We're in removal proceedings — the asylum claim is our defense, and the individual hearing finally got scheduled.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We're in removal — asylum is our defense, and the hearing's finally scheduled.

scenarios.opening_query: The consulate keeps refusing my mother's visitor visa to meet her only grandchild.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: International Law > General Immigration/Visas || Consumer Law > Identity Theft
scenarios.hidden_fact: The refusals cite a 'prior overstay' by someone using her name and birthdate — her passport shows she's never left her country; clearing the stolen-identity hit in the immigration databases is the actual fix.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The refusals cite an overstay by someone using her identity — she's never left her country. Clearing the database hit is the fix.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an identity mix-up poisons the record
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No mix-up — the refusals are standard ties-to-home findings, and a properly documented reapplication is the path.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No mix-up — standard ties-to-home refusals. A documented reapplication is the path.

scenarios.opening_query: My husband's green card renewal was denied over a shoplifting case from before we even met.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The conviction is the whole obstacle — and it was entered without counsel and without the immigration warning the statute requires, which the post-conviction lawyers say makes it vacatable.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The conviction's the obstacle — entered without counsel or the required immigration warning, which makes it vacatable.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the underlying conviction is attackable
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The conviction's solid but minor — the denial misapplied the petty-offense exception, and the immigration motion to reopen is the fix.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The conviction's minor and solid — the denial misapplied the petty-offense exception. Reopen it.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter married abroad and her husband's entry process has stalled past their first anniversary.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: International Law > General Immigration/Visas || International Law > Family Law
scenarios.hidden_fact: The stall exposed something worse — he's already married in his home country and never divorced; my daughter needs the cross-border annulment and its immigration fallout handled together.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The stall surfaced a prior undissolved marriage in his country — she needs the cross-border annulment and its fallout handled together.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a foreign prior marriage voids the case
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No such surprise — the file's stuck on a document-translation rejection, a fixable filing problem.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No surprises — a document-translation rejection is the holdup.
scenarios.vetting_note: Bigamy-discovery routes to international family law.
